note_data.title: Linear Graphs and Their Application
keyPoints: Linear equation in two variables has the general form ax + by + c = 0 where a, b are not both zero
Graph of linear equation in two variables is always a straight line
To draw a graph, find at least two points by substituting values of x and y
x-intercept is the point where line crosses x-axis (y = 0)
y-intercept is the point where line crosses y-axis (x = 0)
Slope of a line is the ratio of vertical change to horizontal change
Two lines are parallel if their slopes are equal
Two lines are perpendicular if product of their slopes is -1
Linear graphs are used in real-life problems like cost analysis, profit calculation, and distance-time relations

definitions.definition: An equation of the form ax + by + c = 0, where a and b are real numbers and both a and b are not zero simultaneously, is called a linear equation in two variables x and y.
The graph of a linear equation in two variables is the set of all points whose coordinates satisfy the equation and it represents a straight line.
The slope of a line is the ratio of the vertical change to the horizontal change between any two points on the line. It is denoted by m and calculated as m = (y₂ - y₁)/(x₂ - x₁).
The x-intercept of a line is the x-coordinate of the point where the line crosses the x-axis. At this point, y = 0.
The y-intercept of a line is the y-coordinate of the point where the line crosses the y-axis. At this point, x = 0.

importantQuestions.question: Define linear equation in two variables and give an example.
importantQuestions.answer: An equation of the form ax + by + c = 0, where a and b are real numbers and both are not zero simultaneously, is called linear equation in two variables. Example: 2x + 3y - 6 = 0

importantQuestions.question: Draw the graph of 2x + y = 4 and find its x-intercept and y-intercept.
importantQuestions.answer: For x-intercept: Put y = 0, so 2x = 4, x = 2. Point is (2,0). For y-intercept: Put x = 0, so y = 4. Point is (0,4). Plot points (2,0), (0,4) and one more point like (1,2). Join these points to get straight line. x-intercept = 2, y-intercept = 4.

mcqs.question: The graph of linear equation in two variables is:
The slope of line passing through points (2,3) and (4,7) is:
If two lines are parallel, then their slopes are:

commonMistakes: Students forget to write 'where a and b are not both zero' in definition of linear equation
Mixing up x-intercept and y-intercept calculations
Not plotting points accurately on graph paper
Forgetting to extend the line beyond plotted points with arrows
Calculating slope incorrectly by reversing the formula
examTips: This chapter usually has 1-2 MCQs and 1 graph drawing question in every BSEK paper
Always draw graphs on proper scale and label axes clearly
Memorize the exact definitions - they are frequently asked
Practice finding intercepts as it appears in every paper
Show all working steps when calculating slope
note_data.keyDiagram: Coordinate plane with x and y axes, showing a straight line passing through two clearly marked points, with x-intercept and y-intercept labeled. Include arrows at both ends of line and proper scaling on axes.
pastPaperRefs: BSEK 2023 SSC-II Q4 (6 marks) — asked students to plot a linear graph from given data and find slope using two points method
BSEK 2022 SSC-II Q7 (4 marks) — required drawing a straight line graph and interpreting the y-intercept from the equation
BSEK 2021 SSC-II Q3 (5 marks) — asked to convert tabular data into linear graph and determine the relationship between variables
BSEK 2020 SSC-II Q6 (3 marks) — tested definition of linear graph and identification of dependent/independent variables

workedExample.question: Draw a linear graph for the equation y = 2x + 3 using values of x from -2 to +2. Find the slope and y-intercept. (7 marks)
steps: Make a table with x values: -2, -1, 0, 1, 2
Plot points (-2,-1), (-1,1), (0,3), (1,5), (2,7) on graph paper with proper scale
Join all points with a straight line using ruler
From equation y = 2x + 3, slope (m) = 2 and y-intercept (c) = 3
workedExample.answer: The linear graph is a straight line passing through the plotted points. Slope = 2, Y-intercept = 3
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for correct table, 2 marks for accurate plotting, 1 mark for straight line, 1 mark for slope, 1 mark for y-intercept, 1 mark for labeling axes

examinerWarnings: Students often lose marks by not using ruler for drawing straight lines. Always use ruler and draw neat, continuous lines through all points.
Many students forget to label axes with proper scales and units. Always write x-axis and y-axis labels with appropriate numbering.
Students frequently confuse slope calculation. Remember: slope = (y₂ - y₁)/(x₂ - x₁) and always show this formula before substituting values.
note_data.quickRecap: Linear graphs are straight line representations of linear equations in the form y = mx + c. The slope (m) shows steepness and direction, while y-intercept (c) is where the line crosses y-axis. Always plot minimum 3 points, use ruler for straight lines, and label axes properly. Remember that positive slope goes upward, negative slope goes downward.
importantFormulas: Y-intercept: c (value of y when x = 0)
X-intercept: value of x when y = 0
